text.title: best-practices-2026 Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for best-practices-2026. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Match AI tool to task type: Copilot for daily inline completion, Cursor for exploratory multi-file edits, Claude Code for documentation / test suites / large refactors.
rule.rationale: Each tool has a different context window, interaction model, and latency profile. Mixing them correctly multiplies productivity; using any single tool for all tasks degrades output quality.

rule.statement: Structure every coding prompt with four parts: Context (stack, constraints), Task (specific action), Requirements (must-haves, edge cases), Output (expected format, file locations).
rule.rationale: Unstructured prompts produce generic code that ignores project conventions. Four-part structure anchors the model to specifics.

rule.statement: Never auto-accept AI-generated code for auth, data access, or business logic. Manual review is required for security-sensitive areas.
rule.rationale: AI can increase defect rates significantly when used without review. Treat AI as assisted driving, not full self-driving.

rule.statement: Iterate: Draft → Review → Refine prompt → Regenerate → Final review. The first AI output is rarely production-ready.
rule.rationale: TBD

rule.statement: Extract relevant clauses from this file into .aidocs/constitution.md for each project. Agents make runtime decisions from the constitution, not from this reference file.
rule.rationale: This file is a 2026 snapshot and ages. The constitution is the versioned, project-specific contract that agents can cite in PRs without risking stale references.

rule.statement: Enable strict + noUncheckedIndexedAccess + exactOptionalPropertyTypes + verbatimModuleSyntax + moduleResolution bundler in every new TypeScript project.
rule.rationale: These flags together catch the most common runtime errors at compile time. Piecemeal adoption creates inconsistent enforcement across the codebase.

rule.statement: Adopt strict tsconfig in one PR. Flip all flags together; piecemeal adoption produces inconsistent enforcement.
rule.rationale: TBD

rule.statement: Use the satisfies operator over type assertion casts for config objects — it validates without widening.
rule.rationale: satisfies catches mismatches at the definition site without losing precise inference, unlike explicit type annotations or as casts.

rule.statement: Use const type parameters to preserve literal types in generic functions instead of widening to base types.
rule.rationale: TBD

rule.statement: Use unknown over any. Use type guards for runtime narrowing. Add explicit return types to all public functions.
rule.rationale: TBD

rule.statement: Default to Server Components. Add 'use client' only when the component needs interactivity (useState, useEffect, event handlers). Push interactivity to leaves.
rule.rationale: Server Components send zero JavaScript to the client and can directly access databases and server-side resources. Overusing 'use client' inflates the bundle and negates RSC benefits.

rule.statement: Server Components can import Client Components. Client Components cannot import Server Components. Children of Client Components become Client Components.
rule.rationale: TBD

rule.statement: Validate all Server Action input with Zod before touching the database. Return { error } shapes for validation failures; never trust formData directly.
rule.rationale: Server Actions run on the server but accept untrusted client data. Zod validation is the only reliable guard.

rule.statement: Use route groups (marketing), parallel routes (@analytics), and intercepting routes ((.)photos) for layout composition without URL pollution.
rule.rationale: TBD

rule.statement: Memoize data fetches shared between generateMetadata and the page component using React cache() to avoid double-fetching.
rule.rationale: Both generateMetadata and the page component may call the same data-fetching function. Without cache(), the request fires twice per render.

rule.statement: Use X | None instead of Optional[X] in all new Python code. Use | union syntax instead of Union[X, Y].
rule.rationale: PEP 604 (Python 3.10+) union syntax is shorter and consistent. Mixing old and new styles in one file creates noise and confuses readers.

rule.statement: Use Protocol for structural typing instead of ABC when consumers define the interface, not the implementer.
rule.rationale: TBD

rule.statement: Use asyncio.TaskGroup (Python 3.11+) for fan-out tasks instead of manual gather + error channels. Wrap with asyncio.timeout to prevent unbounded hangs.
rule.rationale: TaskGroup automatically cancels siblings on first error and propagates exceptions cleanly. Unbounded fan-out without timeout will hang on slow downstream.

rule.statement: Cap async fan-out with asyncio.Semaphore sized to downstream capacity (DB pool, API rate limit), not to CPU count.
rule.rationale: TBD

rule.statement: Evaluate AI testing tools across at least three dimensions before adoption: on-prem availability, pricing model, and lock-in risk. Do not select by recency bias.
rule.rationale: The tool comparison tables in training data are marketing-shaped. Katalon/mabl/testRigor table omits cost, lock-in, and on-prem availability.

rule.statement: Treat AI-generated tests as a first draft. Human review and refinement is mandatory before merging. Never auto-merge AI-generated test files.
rule.rationale: AI test tools generate flaky XPath-anchored tests, duplicate coverage, and cover trivial paths while missing critical ones. The human review step catches all three.

rule.statement: Use role-based locators (ARIA roles, accessible names) in AI-generated UI tests. Reject XPath or CSS selector anchored tests as the primary locator strategy.
rule.rationale: TBD

rule.statement: When using Claude Code to generate tests: specify the test framework, fixture strategy, AAA structure, and edge cases to cover explicitly in the prompt.
rule.rationale: TBD

rule.statement: Project constitution > README. Always copy the relevant clauses into .aidocs/constitution.md with explicit overrides; never have agents read this file at runtime to make decisions.
rule.rationale: The constitution is the versioned, project-specific contract that agents can cite in PRs without risking stale references.

rule.statement: Pin language/runtime versions (engines.node, python_requires) to match the baseline. Drift is silent otherwise.
rule.rationale: TBD

rule.statement: Log structured JSON, request_id everywhere. Both Node (pino) and Python (structlog) sections deserve uniform shape across services.
rule.rationale: TBD

rule.statement: Cite vendor docs over blog posts. When constitution clauses cite react.dev or python.org, agents have a stable source. Medium URLs rot.
rule.rationale: TBD

rule.statement: Run lychee on this README and the constitution monthly. Dead links signal stale guidance.
rule.rationale: TBD
